title: Star Trek Online

cpu: AMD A8-7600 Radeon R7, 10 Compute Cores 4C+6G
notes:
Polling issues with my mouse when right-clicking and moving around, especially wheile walking or running. Disabling Lighting 2.0 has no effect and even when running at medium the performance can be a bit sluggish.

Plus, the menu system when creating a character has low frame rates (about 5 frames per second or so). Performance is better in Windows than in Linux even with the latest kernel and proprietary NVIDIA driver. I'm not sure if using Wine D3D11 has any effect in performance.
title: Star Trek Online

cpu: AMD A8-7600 Radeon R7, 10 Compute Cores 4C+6G
notes: I've had issues dealing with input lag. While in a mission to defeat the Borg in the Vega system, I was trying to target enemy ships and for no reason, the game tries to catch up with mouse movments. I tried to move my mouse quickly while holding down the right mouse button and it will take a second to move left fast. If I overshoot myself, I would move my mouse to the right with the right mouse button down and it will take a couple of seconds to catch up with me and I risk missing the target. This is a major issue and although my previous report is pending, I would have to downgrade down to Bronze status.

title: Star Trek Online

notes: I need to use PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 to launch the game and PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 to be able to click anything. The performance is a lot worse than on Windows (but I can get high FPS on low settings), the controls are so bugged that I can't play (it's often difficult to click a button, sometimes I can't walk, looking around goes crazy after a second). Updating launcher took over an hour (Cryptic Launcher Autoupdate), the game was hard to download (I frequently had to restart the launcher) and it often has connection problems in-game.
title: Star Trek Online

notes: Game takes a very long time to load on "cryptic" and "Age of Discovery" Splash. To get around this launch the game in safe mode and full screen off in the options menu before clicking engage from login screen, then reset graphics once logged in. Plays decently on my potato.
title: Star Trek Online

notes:
Runs bad on even the lowest graphics settings. Disabling Esync only borked clicking on buttons and did nothing otherwise, disabling D3D11 let the game refuse to launch, and using WINE D3D11 slightly improved the performance. Additionally to low framerates, I also had massive input lag. Absolutely unplayable.

# Applies to Proton versions: 3.16-7 Beta, 3.16-4, 3.7-8 Beta, 3.7-8
title: Star Trek Online

notes: I used the command PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 I did not specify 11 or 10. The game ran better when I did this the first time I specified 11 and it had graphical issues. I tried DXVK and it did not run at all. but with Esync disabled and WINED3D it tuns perfect.
title: Star Trek Online

notes:
Audio crackle with lag - fixed by making changes to pulseaudio sample rate and fragments in audio.conf found at https://ludwig.im/en/projects/steam-pulseaudio-sound-latency-lagging-problem-noise

Game will take forever to load after a patch. This is a STO issue not proton.

After audio tweak STO runs perfectly

title: Star Trek Online

notes: Mostly playable since 4.2-2. Minor texture flashing of ground clutter in some maps (New Romulus and Pahvo were the two I noticed it the most). There are some issues with mouse clicks, haven't quite figured out what it is, but disabling Esync seems to have it mostly under control. Game is playable.
title: Star Trek Online

notes:
The launcher and game itself worked out of the box (though the game initially failed to set the right resolution). The only issue which plagues the gameplay is camera panning, which is very jittery and laggy. I attempted to use wine's d3d11 but it had no effect on this issue.
In space mode, the panning issue can make it hard to target vessels, but it is most noticeable with shooter controls in ground mode which are borderline unplayable.

Every other aspect of the game works perfectly though, it's a shame this issue makes it Silver and not Platinum.
title: Star Trek Online

notes: Game loaded, but as soon as I got in and saw the 8-12FPS in ESD I knew it wasn't going to be playable.  I used ''PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=2 %command%' to get it to work, and I also tried again in conjunction to executing Ferall's gamemode script.  Only other note is that the graphics looked really off (lighting was overly dark compared to how it looks in Windows).
title: Star Trek Online

notes: Also got the mouse panning issue which I solved by setting my mouse signal rate to 125 samples/s on a separate mouse profile. This way the emulation layer does not need to process 1000 new mouse inputs every second from my gaming mouse which slowed everything down. Now it runs perfectly, no input lag and good performance on max settings and 3440x1440px.
title: Star Trek Online

notes: Rise of Discovery version of game runs perfectly out of the box, without issues.
title: Star Trek Online

notes:

notes: Couldn't enter any character after login: mouse clicks didn't work with proton 4.11-7,  while it did work on previous versions. mouse was movable, keyboard including tab and esc keys worked. Didn't test with mousekeys. Worked for me before with 4.11-6, runs ok'ish on proton 4.2-11 now (or with some custom builds), hence recommending to try reverting back to a previous version of proton - this may work. Note: I marked it borked only since I couldn't made this specific version work and it was unplayable, but I could make it work and playable with a previous proton version.

notes: This is a correction of a previous report of mine. notes: The latest version of Proton does not work. You'll find the game unable to register mouseclicks. A Google search will tell you to change the polling rate. This is not required, however. Just roll back Proton to 4.2-9
